content: I just finished my latest leather journal cover and I'm obsessed with how it turned out. The burnishing on the edges came out perfectly this time.
That's fantastic! Leatherworking is such a rewarding craft. What type of leather did you use for the journal cover?
Full-grain Horween Chromexcel in natural. I've been buying from the same Chicago supplier for years now. It ages beautifully.
Horween is top-tier leather. I can imagine it developing such a rich patina over time. Do you mostly make journal covers or do you work on other leather goods?

Mostly small accessories - wallets, key fobs, that sort of thing. I tried making a leather backpack once but it was way too ambitious for my skill level. Coco kept trying to chew on the leather scraps while I was working on it.
Yeah, my golden retriever I adopted from Austin Pets Alive last fall. She's a year and a half now and still has puppy energy. Follows me everywhere, including my little workshop in the garage.
Aw, I love golden retrievers! They're such good companions. Does she like watching you work on your leather projects?

She's fascinated by the tools, especially the mallet. I have to keep everything organized and out of reach. Speaking of organization, I need to get better about documenting my leatherworking process for my blog.
You have a blog about your leatherworking? That's impressive! What's it called?

It's called 'River's Hide & Seek' - mostly about leathercraft but I throw in travel stories too. Just got back from a month in Colombia where I visited a family-run tannery outside Medellín. Fascinating process.
That sounds incredible! I'd love to read about that experience. Do you speak Spanish? The tannery visit must have been quite an adventure.

Sí, hablo español bastante bien. Lived in Buenos Aires for a year back in my twenties. The tannery owners didn't speak much English, so it was perfect practice. Plus, I got to dance salsa while I was there - my other great love besides leather.
You're living the dream! Leatherworking, travel, salsa dancing - such a rich life. Do you have a favorite salsa club in Austin?

El Bosque is my go-to, though it's been too hot lately to dance much. Coco doesn't love being left alone at night either. Maybe I'll take her to a dog-friendly brewery instead this weekend and work on some leather keychains while I'm there.
That sounds like the perfect weekend plan! Coco gets to socialize and you get to craft. Do you sell your leather goods anywhere or is it just a hobby?

Mostly just for friends and family, though I did sell some pieces at the Violet Crown Art Fair last December. Made enough to fund my leather supply addiction for a few months. The Chromexcel isn't cheap!
